original_poem:
वीणा वादिनी, वर दे!  
प्रिय स्वतंत्र-रवी, वर दे!
उज्ज्वल गगन का छोर, चीर  
अग्नि-बाण सा सब ओर वीर  
विकट प्रलय का कर नाद,  
खोल दे अब मृतिका के जाल।

धधकता रहे, यह ह्रदय  
प्रज्वलित प्राणों में, फिर से  
गाए यह जीवन वीणा,  
प्रलय में भी अमर हो जाना।
translated_poem:
O Lute of Fire, grant us your boon!  
Bless us, dear light of freedom's noon!  
Pierce the radiant sky, like flame,  
A hero’s call, fearless in name,  
Roar with a fury, with thunder’s might,  
Break the chains of death’s endless night.

Let this heart burn, with passion aglow,  
And through all lives, let courage grow,  
Let the lute of life sing on,  
Even through the storm, let it live on.

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

source_lines: खोल दे अब मृतिका के जाल।
translated_lines: O Lute of Fire, grant us your boon!
Bless us, dear light of freedom's noon!
Pierce the radiant sky, like flame,
A hero’s call, fearless in name,
Roar with a fury, with thunder’s might,
Break the chains of death’s endless night.

source_lines: प्रलय में भी अमर हो जाना।
translated_lines: Let this heart burn, with passion aglow,
And through all lives, let courage grow,
Let the lute of life sing on,
Even through the storm, let it live on.

emotional_arc.value: Opens with a fierce invocation for freedom's fire to pierce the sky and shatter death's chains, then deepens into a vow of undying passion and immortal song even through destruction

loss_note.value: मृतिका के जाल loses its specific earthy connotation of clay-bound mortality; rendered generically as death's chains.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: freedom's call as a flaming arrow piercing oppressive darkness
the entrapment of mortal, clay-bound existence that must be broken
invoked divine source of inspired speech and power
freedom imagined as a radiant sun
revolutionary force shooting across all directions

a cataclysmic roar breaking earthen nets
a flaming arrow shooting across a radiant open sky in all directions
a hero's figure roaring amid thunderous storm clouds
chains of clay shattering under a blazing light

Second use of pralaya as cosmic annihilation rendered as generic storm, losing eschatological weight.
